strategy: Coordinate entry so everyone is in by Round 2. Any wizard who joins after Round 2 is interrupt-hit by a Shadow Insane Bolt for 2,000 damage and loses 3 pips. Have your slowest member port out and Dungeon Recall to control battle order.

Kill the Spiderling first. On Round 2 it casts a permanent Shadow Sentinel that intercepts 90% of your damage to the boss and gives it -30% incoming, so hits barely land until the minion is gone. Stack Feints on the Spiderling and finish it with a 4-5 pip spell.

Beat the Feint-swap with Melt. Feinting the boss makes him cast Swap Ward, flipping your 70% trap onto you as a weak one. Cast Melt first to strip a shield, or use an Indemnity Feint or item/pet/TC Feint - none of those get swapped.

Run the universal 5-round setup: R1 melt/melt/trap/trap (add a prism if you are Storm), R2 Feint the minion x3 and kill it, R3 feint/feint/trap/trap, R4 Indemnity Feint then blades, R5 blades then main hit.

Blade and hit before Dimension Shift fires. He steals every positive charm and ward from all wizards every 8/7/6/5 rounds for 1/2/3/4 wizards. Time your buff-and-hit to land inside that window so the steal grabs nothing.

Pierce his resistances. He resists Storm 40% and Fire/Ice/Life/Death/Balance 20%, but boosts Myth 10%. Attack with Myth if you can, otherwise add Infallible to punch through. Bring Doom/infection because his 'Surprised?' cheat self-heals with Healing Current.

Do not place Bad Juju on him - it makes him interrupt-cast Sacrifice on himself. Keep your setup to blades, traps, and Feints (via Melt) and end the fight in one big AoE so the Spiderling dies with him.
cheatNotes: Latecomers are interrupt-hit by a Shadow Insane Bolt for 2,000 damage and lose 3 pips, so make sure everyone is in the duel by the end of Round 2.

On Round 1 he summons a Spiderling that becomes active Round 2 and casts a permanent Shadow Sentinel (90% intercept, +80 block, -30% incoming), which is why the minion must die before you try to hit the boss.
Kill the Spiderling and he simply summons a replacement three rounds later, so plan to clear it early rather than repeatedly.
Only one Spiderling exists at a time, so you never have to deal with more than a single active minion.
He only summons as many total Spiderlings as there are wizards (four total in a full team); once that many are dead, his summon attempts fail every three rounds.
Every third round he swaps who goes first, so a plan that relies on hitting before or after him can flip - account for both orders.
His Shadow Dimension Shift strips all positive charms and wards from the whole team, so you must blade and hit in the same window rather than banking buffs across rounds.
With one wizard the steal fires every 8 rounds.
With two wizards the steal fires every 7 rounds.
With three wizards the steal fires every 6 rounds.
With four wizards the steal fires every 5 rounds, the tightest window, so a full team must burst fast.
His 'Surprised?' cheat hits a random wizard with his Natural Attack and then heals himself with Healing Current, so bring Doom/Gloom or an infection if the fight drags.
When he says 'You had your chance,' he casts Iron Sultan as a large attack - be topped up on health going into long fights.
Placing a Feint on him triggers Swap Ward, moving your trap value onto you, so never Feint him raw.
You can avoid the Swap-Ward by using an Indemnity Feint or by casting Melt first, since Melt-protected traps and indemnified Feints cannot be swapped.
Placing Bad Juju on him makes him interrupt-cast Sacrifice on himself, so keep Bad Juju out of your deck for this fight.
